frontmatter.title: The internal category associated to a displayed object

mainmatter.p: Let \bar {u} be a displayed object in a locally small fibration E over B. We will define the internal category { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } in B associated to \bar {u}. In particular, we let the object of objects { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } _{ 0 } be u itself; defining the object of arrows { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } _{ 1 } is more complex, making critical use of the local smallness of E over B.

We will think of the fiber category E _{ u \times u } as the category of objects indexed in the boundary (source and target) of a morphism. Restricting \bar {u} along the source and target projections, we obtain the displayed objects of “points of the source” and “points of the target” respectively:

Because E is locally small, there is an object \mathbf {hom} _{ E _{ u \times u } } { \left ( \bar \partial _{ 1 } , \bar \partial _{ 2 } \right )} \in { B } _{ / u \times u } that behaves as the “generic hom set”. We define { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } _{ 1 } \in B and its source and target projections to be this very object.

frontmatter.title: The full internal subcategory associated to a displayed object

mainmatter.p: The full subfibration associated to a displayed object \bar {u} of a locally small cartesian fibration E over B can be seen to be equivalent to the externalization of an internal category { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } in B.

The externalization { \left [ { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } \right ]} of the internal category { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } associated to a displayed object \bar {u} in a locally small fibered category E over B is equivalent to the full internal subfibration \mathbf {Full} { \left ( \bar {u} \right )}.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: We will define a fibred equivalence F : { \left [ { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } \right ]} \to \mathbf {Full} { \left ( \bar {u} \right )} over B.

ol.li: Fix x \in B and \chi _{ x } \in { \left [ { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } \right ]} _{ x }, i.e. \chi _{ x } : x \to u; we define F { \left ( \chi _{ x } \right )} to be an arbitrary cartesian map \phi _{ x } : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ \chi _{ x } ]{} \bar {u}. (Here we have used the axiom of choice for collections.)

li.p: Fix f : x \to y \in B and \chi _{ x } :x \to u and \chi _{ y } :y \to u and a diagram representing a displayed morphism h from \chi _{ x } to \chi _{ y } over f as below:

We must define F { \left ( h \right )} : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ f ]{} \bar {y}, fixing arbitrary cartesian maps \bar \chi _{ x } : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ \chi _{ x } ]{} \bar {u} and \bar \chi _{ y } : \bar {y} \xrightarrow [ \chi _{ y } ]{} \bar {u}. First we lift h:x \to { \left [ { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } \right ]} _{ 1 } into E using the universal property of the cartesian lift:
By composition with the “evaluation map” for our hom object, we have a map \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ f; \chi _{ y } ]{} \bar {u}:
Next we define F { \left ( h \right )} : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ f ]{} \bar {y} using the universal property of (another) cartesian lift:

mainmatter.p: Let E be a category with finite limits; then an internal categoryin E is defined by the following data:

ol.li: an object of objects C _{ 0 } \in E,
an object of morphisms C _{ 1 } \in E,
source and target morphisms s,t:C _{ 1 } \to C _{ 0 },
a generic identity morphism C _{ 0 } \to C _{ 1 },
a generic composition morphism C _{ 1 } \times _{ C _{ 0 } } C _{ 1 } \to C _{ 1 },
satisfying a number of laws corresponding to those of a category.

mainmatter.p: For the details of these laws, we refer to any standard source.

A cartesian fibration E over B is locally small if and only if for each x \in B and u,v \in E _{ x }, the total category \widetilde { \mathbf {H}_{ E _{ x } }( u , v ) } of hom candidateshas a terminal object. Viewed as a displayed object in E, we shall write \overline { \mathbf {hom}} _{ E _{ x } } ( u , v ) lying over \mathbf {hom} _{ E _{ x } } { \left ( u , v \right )} for the terminal hom candidate.

The externalization { \left [ { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } \right ]} of the internal category { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } associated to a displayed object \bar {u} in a locally small fibered category E over B is equivalent to the full internal subfibration \mathbf {Full} { \left ( \bar {u} \right )}.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: We will define a fibred equivalence F : { \left [ { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } \right ]} \to \mathbf {Full} { \left ( \bar {u} \right )} over B.

ol.li: Fix x \in B and \chi _{ x } \in { \left [ { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } \right ]} _{ x }, i.e. \chi _{ x } : x \to u; we define F { \left ( \chi _{ x } \right )} to be an arbitrary cartesian map \phi _{ x } : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ \chi _{ x } ]{} \bar {u}. (Here we have used the axiom of choice for collections.)

li.p: Fix f : x \to y \in B and \chi _{ x } :x \to u and \chi _{ y } :y \to u and a diagram representing a displayed morphism h from \chi _{ x } to \chi _{ y } over f as below:

We must define F { \left ( h \right )} : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ f ]{} \bar {y}, fixing arbitrary cartesian maps \bar \chi _{ x } : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ \chi _{ x } ]{} \bar {u} and \bar \chi _{ y } : \bar {y} \xrightarrow [ \chi _{ y } ]{} \bar {u}. First we lift h:x \to { \left [ { \left \langle \bar {u} \right \rangle } \right ]} _{ 1 } into E using the universal property of the cartesian lift:
By composition with the “evaluation map” for our hom object, we have a map \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ f; \chi _{ y } ]{} \bar {u}:
Next we define F { \left ( h \right )} : \bar {x} \xrightarrow [ f ]{} \bar {y} using the universal property of (another) cartesian lift:

frontmatter.title: The internalization of a small fibration

mainmatter.p: Let E be a small fibration over B a category with finite limits, i.e. a cartesian fibration that is both locally small and globally small. We will show that E is equivalent to the externalization { \left [ C \right ]} of an internal category C in B, namely the full internal subcategory associated to the generic object \bar {u} \in E.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: By the characterization of the externalization we know that the externalization of C so-defined is equivalent to the full subfibration \mathbf {Full} { \left ( \bar {u} \right )} of E spanned by objects that are “classified” by \bar {u}. Because \bar {u} is generic, we know that every object of E is classified by \bar {u}, so we are done.
